#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>

struct NetworkPacket {

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>

struct NetworkPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string username;
    std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
    std::string metadata;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << packet_id;
        stream << hexi::length_adaptor(username);
        stream << hexi::length_adaptor(scores);
        stream << hexi::null_terminated(metadata);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_packet(const NetworkPacket& packet) {
    hexi::DynamicBuffer buffer;
    hexi::BufferAdaptor adaptor(buffer);
    packet.serialise(adaptor);
    return buffer.data();
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct NetworkPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string username;
    std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
    std::string metadata;
};

NetworkPacket deserialize_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    NetworkPacket packet;
    
    // Read packet_id
    hexi::read<uint32_t>(buffer, packet.packet_id);
    
    // Read username (prefixed)
    hexi::read< hexi::prefixed<std::string> >(buffer, packet.username);
    
    // Read scores count
    uint32_t count;
    hexi::read<uint32_t>(buffer, count);
    
    // Read scores vector
    packet.scores.resize(count);
    hexi::read_container(buffer, packet.scores);
    
    // Read metadata (null-terminated)
    hexi::read< hexi::null_terminated<std::string> >(buffer, packet.metadata);
    
    // Skip padding
    hexi::skip(buffer, buffer.size());
    
    return packet;
}

struct PacketInfo {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string_view username;
};

PacketInfo extract_packet_info(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    PacketInfo info;
    
    // Use span() for packet_id (zero-copy)
    info.packet_id = *reinterpret_cast<const uint32_t*>(buffer.data());
    
    // Read username using prefixed adaptor (string_view)
    hexi::read< hexi::prefixed<std::string_view> >(buffer, info.username);
    
    // Read scores count
    uint32_t count;
    hexi::read<uint32_t>(buffer, count);
    
    // Skip scores data
    hexi::skip(buffer, count * sizeof(uint32_t));
    
    // Skip metadata (null-terminated)
    hexi::skip_until(buffer, 0x00);
    hexi::skip(buffer, 1); // skip null terminator
    
    // Skip padding
    hexi::skip(buffer, buffer.size());
    
    return info;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct MessageHeader {
    uint16_t message_type;
    uint32_t body_length;
    uint32_t footer;
};

MessageHeader read_header_and_skip_body(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    MessageHeader header;
    header.message_type = hexi::read_uint16(buffer.data(), 0);
    header.body_length = hexi::read_uint32(buffer.data(), 2);
    hexi::skip(buffer.data(), 6, header.body_length);
    header.footer = hexi::read_uint32(buffer.data(), 6 + header.body_length);
    return header;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

std::vector<uint32_t> read_item_batch(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {

}

DataResult preview_and_read_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    DataResult result;
    
    // Check if buffer has at least data_type (1 byte) + item_count (4 bytes)
    if (buffer.size() < 5) {
        return result;
    }
    
    // Preview data_type without advancing read pointer
    result.data_type = buffer[0];
    
    // Create a sub-span starting after data_type
    std::span<const uint8_t> sub_buffer(buffer.data() + 1, buffer.size() - 1);
    
    // Create buffer_adaptor and binary_stream for reading item_count and items
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(sub_buffer.data(), sub_buffer.size());
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);
    
    // Read item_count
    uint32_t item_count;
    stream.read(&item_count, sizeof(item_count));
    
    // Check if buffer has enough data for items
    size_t required_total_size = 5 + item_count * 4;
    if (buffer.size() < required_total_size) {
        return result;
    }
    
    // Read items based on data_type
    if (result.data_type == 0) {
        result.int_items.resize(item_count);
        stream.read(result.int_items.data(), item_count * sizeof(uint32_t));
    } else {
        result.float_items.resize(item_count);
        stream.read(result.float_items.data(), item_count * sizeof(float));
    }
    
    return result;
}
```

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct DataResult {
    uint8_t data_type;
    std::vector<uint32_t> int_items;
    std::vector<float> float_items;
};

DataResult preview_and_read_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    DataResult result;
    
    // Check if buffer has at least data_type (1 byte) + item_count (4 bytes)
    if (buffer.size() < 5) {
        return result;
    }
    
    // Preview data_type without advancing read pointer
    result.data_type = buffer[0];
    
    // Create a sub-span starting after data_type
    std::span<const uint8_t> sub_buffer(buffer.data() + 1, buffer.size() - 1);
    
    // Create buffer_adaptor and binary_stream for reading item_count and items
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(sub_buffer.data(), sub_buffer.size());
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);
    
    // Read item_count
    uint32_t item_count;
    stream.read(&item_count, sizeof(item_count));
    
    // Check if buffer has enough data for items
    size_t required_total_size = 5 + item_count * 4;
    if (buffer.size() < required_total_size) {
        return result;
    }
    
    // Read items based on data_type
    if (result.data_type == 0) {
        result.int_items.resize(item_count);
        stream.read(result.int_items.data(), item_count * sizeof(uint32_t));
    } else {
        result.float_items.resize(item_count);
        stream.read(result.float_items.data(), item_count * sizeof(float));
    }
    
    return result;
}

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

bool write_data_with_check(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, uint32_t header, const std::vector<uint32_t>& items) {
    // Calculate required space: header (4 bytes) + items (each 4 bytes)
    size_t required_space = sizeof(uint32_t) + items.size() * sizeof(uint32_t);
    
    // Check if buffer has sufficient space
    if (buffer.size() < required_space) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // Use buffer_adaptor to interface with the buffer
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);
    
    // Write header and items
    stream.write(header);
    stream.write_container(items);
    
    return true;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct PacketHeader {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint16_t version;
    uint16_t flags;
};

PacketHeader read_header_with_span(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // Create a buffer adaptor from the span's data and size
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<const uint8_t> adaptor(buffer.data(), buffer.size());

        PacketHeader default_header{0, 0, 0};
        return default_header;
    }
    
    // Create a binary stream for reading from the buffer adaptor
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);
    
    // Read the header fields directly from the buffer using zero-copy operations
    PacketHeader header;
    stream.read(&header.magic, sizeof(header.magic));
    stream.read(&header.version, sizeof(header.version));
    stream.read(&header.flags, sizeof(header.flags));
    
    return header;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <string>

struct UserData {
    uint32_t user_id;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& adaptor) {
        adaptor(user_id, name);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> write_and_serialise_packet(uint32_t header, const UserData& data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::Adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    adaptor(header);
    data.serialise(adaptor);
    return buffer;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <bit>

template<typename T>
void convert_to_big_endian_inplace(T& value) {
    hexi::endian::native_to_big(value);
}

template<typename T>
void convert_to_little_endian_inplace(T& value) {
    hexi::endian::native_to_little(value);
}

template<std::endian from, std::endian to, typename T>
void convert_endianness_inplace(T& value) {
    if constexpr (from != to) {
        if constexpr (from == std::endian::big && to == std::endian::little) {
            hexi::endian::big_to_little(value);
        } else if constexpr (from == std::endian::little && to == std::endian::big) {
            hexi::endian::little_to_big(value);

            hexi::endian::native_to_big(value);
        } else if constexpr (from == std::endian::native && to == std::endian::little) {
            hexi::endian::native_to_little(value);
        } else if constexpr (from == std::endian::big && to == std::endian::native) {
            hexi::endian::big_to_native(value);
        } else if constexpr (from == std::endian::little && to == std::endian::native) {
            hexi::endian::little_to_native(value);
        }
    }
}
```</answer>

template<hexi::endian::conversion conv, typename T>

        return value;

}

#include <hexi/file_buffer.h>
#include <vector>

#include <filesystem>

std::vector<uint8_t> read_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, size_t bytes_to_read) {
    hexi::file_buffer fb;
    fb.open(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::mode::read);
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer(bytes_to_read);
    size_t bytes_read = fb.read(buffer.data(), bytes_to_read);
    buffer.resize(bytes_read);
    return buffer;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> preview_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, size_t bytes_to_preview) {
    hexi::file_buffer fb;
    fb.open(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::mode::read);
    auto current_pos = fb.tell();
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer(bytes_to_preview);
    size_t bytes_read = fb.read(buffer.data(), bytes_to_preview);
    buffer.resize(bytes_read);
    fb.seek(current_pos);
    return buffer;
}

void write_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) {
    hexi::file_buffer fb;
    fb.open(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::mode::write);
    fb.write(data.data(), data.size());
}

size_t get_file_size(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    hexi::file_buffer fb;
    fb.open(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::mode::read);
    fb.seek(0, hexi::file_buffer::seek::end);
    return fb.tell();
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/file_buffer.h>
#include <filesystem>

size_t get_file_readable_size(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    hexi::file_buffer buf(file_path);
    if (!buf.error()) {
        return buf.size();
    }
    return 0;
}

bool is_file_buffer_valid(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    hexi::file_buffer buf(file_path);
    return !buf.error();
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
void write_data_to_buffer(hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) {
    for (size_t i = 0; i < data.size() && i < buffer_size; ++i) {
        buffer[i] = static_cast<std::byte>(data[i]);
    }
}

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
std::vector<uint8_t> read_data_from_buffer(hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer, size_t bytes_to_read) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> result;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < bytes_to_read && i < buffer_size; ++i) {

#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct UserData {
    uint32_t user_id;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) const {
        stream << user_id << name;
    }
};

void serialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const UserData& data) {
    data.serialise(writer);
}

void deserialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader, UserData& data) {
    data.serialise(reader);
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <string>

void write_string_to_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const std::string& str) {
    writer.write_uint32(static_cast<uint32_t>(str.length()));
    writer.write_bytes(str.data(), str.length());
}

std::string read_string_from_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    uint32_t length = reader.read_uint32();
    std::string result;
    result.resize(length);
    reader.read_bytes(result.data(), length);
    return result;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstring>

void peek_data_from_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, void* data, std::size_t length) {
    buffer.copy(data, length);
}

template<typename T>
void peek_value_from_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, T& value) {
    buffer.copy(value);
}

bool peek_and_compare(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, const void* expected, std::size_t length) {
    std::vector<std::byte> temp(length);
    buffer.copy(temp.data(), length);
    
    const std::byte* exp = static_cast<const std::byte*>(expected);
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < length; ++i) {
        if (temp[i] != exp[i]) {
            return false;
        }
    }
    return true;
}
```</answer>

}

void seek_write_position(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, hexi::buffer_seek direction, std::size_t offset) {
    buffer.write_seek(direction, offset);
}

    // Save current position
    auto saved_pos = buffer.tell_write();

    buffer.write_seek(hexi::buffer_seek::sk_absolute, 0);
    const std::byte* header_bytes = reinterpret_cast<const std::byte*>(&header_value);
    buffer.write(header_bytes, sizeof(header_value));

    std::string body = "This is the body data";
    buffer.write(reinterpret_cast<const std::byte*>(body.data()), body.size());
    
    // Seek back to saved position
    buffer.write_seek(hexi::buffer_seek::sk_absolute, saved_pos);
    
    // Seek back to beginning and update header
    buffer.write_seek(hexi::buffer_seek::sk_absolute, 0);
    buffer.write(header_bytes, sizeof(header_value));
}
```

#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <type_traits>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<typename T>
std::size_t encode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, T value) {
    using UnsignedT = typename std::make_unsigned<T>::type;
    UnsignedT unsigned_value;
    
    if constexpr (std::is_signed_v<T>) {
        UnsignedT zigzag = (static_cast<UnsignedT>(value) << 1) ^ (static_cast<UnsignedT>(value) >> (sizeof(T) * 8 - 1));
        unsigned_value = zigzag;
    } else {
        unsigned_value = static_cast<UnsignedT>(value);
    }
    
    std::size_t bytes_written = 0;
    while (unsigned_value > 0x7F) {
        uint8_t byte = (unsigned_value & 0x7F) | 0x80;
        writer.write_byte(byte);
        unsigned_value >>= 7;
        bytes_written++;
    }
    uint8_t byte = unsigned_value & 0x7F;
    writer.write_byte(byte);
    return ++bytes_written;
}

template<typename T>
T decode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    using UnsignedT = typename std::make_unsigned<T>::type;
    UnsignedT result = 0;
    int shift = 0;
    
    while (true) {
        uint8_t byte = reader.read_byte();
        result |= static_cast<UnsignedT>(byte & 0x7F) << shift;
        if ((byte & 0x80) == 0) {
            break;
        }
        shift += 7;
        if (shift >= sizeof(T) * 8) {
            throw std::overflow_error("Varint too large");
        }
    }
    
    if constexpr (std::is_signed_v<T>) {
        T signed_result = static_cast<T>((result >> 1) ^ (-(result & 1)));
        return signed_result;
    } else {
        return static_cast<T>(result);
    }
}

template<typename T>
bool encode_decode_roundtrip(T original_value) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer writer(adaptor);
    hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader reader(adaptor);
    
    std::size_t bytes_written = encode_varint(writer, original_value);
    reader.seek(0); // Reset reader to start
    
    T decoded_value = decode_varint<T>(reader);
    return decoded_value == original_value;
}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <string_view>

void write_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream, const std::string& str) {
    stream << hexi::prefixed_varint(str);
}

std::string read_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream) {
    std::string result;
    stream >> hexi::prefixed_varint(result);
    return result;
}
```</answer>

<think>Okay, let's tackle this problem. The user wants me to use the Hexi library to create two functions: one that generates a filled array at compile time and another to verify it. 

First, looking at the `create_filled_array` function. It's supposed to be a constexpr function, so it needs to be evaluated at compile time. The Hexi library probably has some utilities for this. I remember that Hexi has a `generate_filled` function or something similar. Let me check the documentation in my mind. Oh right, Hexi's `detail` namespace might have a `generate_filled` function that takes size and value and returns a filled array.

Wait, the function signature is `constexpr auto create_filled_array(std::uint8_t value)`. The template parameter is the size. So the function should return an array of size `Size` filled with `value`. Using Hexi's `generate_filled` would make sense here. So the implementation would be something like `return generate_filled<Size>(value);`.

Then the verification function. The user wants to check if all elements in the array are equal to the expected value. Since the array could be of any type (like std::array<uint8_t, N>), we need to iterate through each element. But since this isn't constexpr, we can use a loop. For each element in the array, check if it equals the expected value. If any element doesn't match, return false. Otherwise, return true.

Wait, but how do we handle different array types? The function uses `auto& arr`, which is perfect because it can take any array type. Then, using a range-based for loop would work. So inside the loop, for each element in arr, compare to expected_value. If any element is not equal, return false. After checking all, return true.

Putting it all together. The first function uses Hexi's generate_filled, and the second loops through the array. Let me make sure there's no syntax errors. Also, include the necessary headers: hexi/shared.h, array, and uint8_t.

So the code would look like:

For create_filled_array, return generate_filled<Size>(value). And verify_filled_array loops through each element. That should do it.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <array>
#include <cstdint>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<std::size_t Size>
constexpr auto create_filled_array(std::uint8_t value) {
    return generate_filled<Size>(value);
}

bool verify_filled_array(const auto& arr, std::uint8_t expected_value) {
    for (const auto& element : arr) {
        if (element != expected_value) {
            return false;
        }
    }
    return true;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct CompactPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::vector<std::string> tags;
    std::string data;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_compact_packet(const CompactPacket& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    
    // Serialize packet_id as varint
    auto id_encoded = hexi::varint_encode(packet.packet_id);
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), id_encoded.begin(), id_encoded.end());
    
    // Serialize tags using prefixed_varint adaptor for vector<string>
    hexi::prefixed_varint<std::vector<std::string>> tags_adaptor;
    auto tags_encoded = tags_adaptor.serialize(packet.tags);
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), tags_encoded.begin(), tags_encoded.end());
    
    // Serialize data using prefixed_varint adaptor for string
    hexi::prefixed_varint<std::string> data_adaptor;
    auto data_encoded = data_adaptor.serialize(packet.data);
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), data_encoded.begin(), data_encoded.end());
    
    return buffer;
}

CompactPacket deserialize_compact_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    CompactPacket packet;
    size_t pos = 0;
    
    // Deserialize packet_id
    packet.packet_id = hexi::varint_decode<uint32_t>(buffer, pos);
    
    // Deserialize tags
    hexi::prefixed_varint<std::vector<std::string>> tags_adaptor;
    packet.tags = tags_adaptor.deserialize(buffer, pos);
    
    // Deserialize data
    hexi::prefixed_varint<std::string> data_adaptor;
    packet.data = data_adaptor.deserialize(buffer, pos);
    
    return packet;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <vector>

struct PaddedData {
    uint16_t header;
    std::string data;
    uint32_t footer;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_with_padding(const PaddedData& packet) {
    hexi::allocators::default_allocator allocator;
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::stream stream(allocator, adaptor);
    
    // Write header
    stream.write(packet.header);
    
    // Write data length as uint32_t
    stream.write(static_cast<uint32_t>(packet.data.size()));
    
    // Write data string
    stream.write(packet.data);
    
    // Write 16 bytes of padding (0x00)
    stream.fill<16>(0x00);
    
    // Write footer
    stream.write(packet.footer);
    
    return buffer;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <vector>
#include <memory>

struct PoolObject {
    int id;
    double value;
    
    // Default constructor required by block_allocator
    PoolObject() : id(0), value(0.0) {}
    
    PoolObject(int i, double v) : id(i), value(v) {}
};

std::vector<PoolObject*> create_object_pool(hexi::block_allocator<PoolObject, 10>& allocator) {
    std::vector<PoolObject*> objects;
    
    // Allocate and construct the first object
    PoolObject* obj1 = allocator.allocate();
    new (obj1) PoolObject(1, 3.14);
    objects.push_back(obj1);
    
    // Allocate and construct the second object
    PoolObject* obj2 = allocator.allocate();
    new (obj2) PoolObject(2, 6.28);
    objects.push_back(obj2);
    
    // Allocate and construct the third object
    PoolObject* obj3 = allocator.allocate();
    new (obj3) PoolObject(3, 9.42);
    objects.push_back(obj3);
    
    return objects;
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_base.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstddef>

class CustomBuffer : public hexi::pmc::buffer_base {
    std::vector<std::byte> data_;
    
public:
    // Implement required virtual methods from buffer_base
    std::size_t size() const override {
        return data_.size();
    }
    
    bool empty() const override {
        return data_.empty();
    }
    
    // Helper method to add data (for testing)
    void add_data(const void* source, std::size_t length) {
        const std::byte* bytes = static_cast<const std::byte*>(source);
        data_.insert(data_.end(), bytes, bytes + length);
    }
    
    // Helper method to clear data (for testing)
    void clear() {
        data_.clear();
    }
};
```</answer>

#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::vector<uint8_t> write_and_read_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& input_data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor writer(buffer);
    
    // Check available space before writing
    size_t free_space = writer.free();
    if (free_space < input_data.size()) {
        buffer.resize(input_data.size());
    }
    
    writer.write(input_data.data(), input_data.size());
    
    // Verify the size after writing
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor reader(buffer);
    size_t readable_size = reader.size();
    
    // Read back the data
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(readable_size);
    reader.read(result.data(), readable_size);
    
    return result;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> peek_data_without_advancing(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer_data, std::size_t peek_size) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer_data);
    
    // Get pointer to current read position
    const uint8_t* read_ptr = adaptor.read_ptr();
    
    // Copy data without advancing the read pointer
    std::vector<uint8_t> peeked_data(read_ptr, read_ptr + peek_size);
    
    // Read to consume the data (advance the pointer)
    adaptor.read(nullptr, peek_size);
    
    return peeked_data;
}
```</answer>

}

std::vector<uint8_t> read_partial_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, std::size_t chunk_size) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_sequence buf(buffer);
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buf);
    std::vector<uint8_t> result;
    
    while (adaptor.size() > 0) {
        std::vector<uint8_t> chunk(chunk_size);
        std::size_t bytes_read = adaptor.read(chunk.data(), chunk_size);
        chunk.resize(bytes_read);
        result.insert(result.end(), chunk.begin(), chunk.end());
    }
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

    uint8_t header_data[sizeof(Header)];

    if (first_byte == 0xFF) {

    }
    return result;
}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct ProtocolPacket {
    uint32_t message_id;
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
    uint32_t checksum;
};

struct ProtocolHeader {
    uint32_t message_id;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_protocol_packet(const ProtocolPacket& packet) {
    hexi::memory_buffer buffer;
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer);
    
    // Convert and write message_id
    uint32_t be_message_id = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.message_id);
    stream.write(be_message_id);
    
    // Convert and write data_values
    std::vector<uint16_t> be_data_values;
    for (auto val : packet.data_values) {
        be_data_values.push_back(hexi::endian::native_to_big(val));
    }
    stream.write_container(be_data_values);
    
    // Convert and write checksum
    uint32_t be_checksum = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.checksum);
    stream.write(be_checksum);
    
    return buffer.data();
}

ProtocolPacket deserialize_protocol_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::memory_buffer input_buffer(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    hexi::binary_stream stream(input_buffer);
    
    ProtocolPacket packet;
    
    // Read and convert message_id
    uint32_t be_message_id;
    stream.read(be_message_id);
    packet.message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_message_id);
    
    // Read and convert data_values count
    uint32_t be_count;
    stream.read(be_count);
    uint32_t count = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_count);
    
    // Read and convert data_values
    std::vector<uint16_t> be_data_values(count);
    stream.read_container(be_data_values);
    packet.data_values.resize(count);
    for (size_t i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
        packet.data_values[i] = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_data_values[i]);
    }
    
    // Read and convert checksum
    uint32_t be_checksum;
    stream.read(be_checksum);
    packet.checksum = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_checksum);
    
    return packet;
}

ProtocolHeader extract_protocol_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::memory_buffer input_buffer(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    hexi::binary_stream stream(input_buffer);
    
    // Get span of message_id and convert
    auto header_span = stream.span(4);
    uint32_t be_message_id = *reinterpret_cast<const uint32_t*>(header_span.data());
    uint32_t message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_message_id);
    
    // Skip the header
    stream.skip(4);
    
    return {message_id};
}
```</answer>
